basics.name: François-Pierre Paty
basics.label: Applied mathematics · Machine learning · Optimal transport · Time series · Operations research
basics.summary: Applied mathematician (PhD, École Polytechnique engineer) working at the intersection of machine learning, optimization and operations research. I built the entire R&D of an AI startup (Ida, acquired by RELEX Solutions) from scratch, published at top machine-learning venues (ICML, AISTATS) on algorithmic optimal transport, and run a Chinese-learning platform and newsletter that reach more than 25,000 people worldwide.

highlights: Teaching mathematics (analysis, probability, differential calculus and equations) in French to Chinese students: lectures, tutorials and oral examinations (khôlles).
Distilling and presenting technical material to varied audiences, and collaborating in a multicultural, Chinese-speaking environment.

experience.org: Ida (acquired by RELEX Solutions in 2025, now RELEX Fresh)
highlights: Startup optimizing fresh-food orders for grocery retailers. Built the entire R&D pipeline from scratch: research, implementation, production deployment and fast iteration in close contact with field data and data engineers.
Impact: 30–50% reduction in food waste, 3 margin points gained, deployment across hundreds of stores in Europe.
Modeling: time-series forecasting (ML), stock reliability (HMM), order optimization (MILP, MDP, dynamic programming).
Production: deployment and monitoring in Python, Git and CI/CD.
Mentored MSc-level data-science interns; led technical presentations to clients and in tenders.
Chinese-proficiency testing platform (120,000+ tests, 5M answers) and a Substack Bestseller newsletter with 25,000+ subscribers worldwide.
Full-stack development, algorithmic modeling and AI-assisted app building.
Growing recurring revenue (+50% in 2025); building a new app integrating generative AI.
Developed novel machine-learning algorithms with applications in economics; optimal transport applied to labor-market modeling, with numerical simulations in Python (JAX).

experience.org: ENSAE Paris, Institut Polytechnique de Paris
highlights: Algorithmic optimal transport under the supervision of Marco Cuturi (CREST funding).
3 publications (ICML ×2, AISTATS) including a Notable Paper Award; 13 talks at international conferences and seminars.
Teaching assistant in mathematics, optimization, machine learning and deep learning (BSc and MSc levels).

experience.org: EDF R&D & Finance for Energy Market Research Centre
highlights: Adapted sparse-deconvolution techniques to missing-data imputation for financial time series. Received the congratulations of the Applied Mathematics department of École polytechnique; a patent was filed by EDF (FR3075969B1).

education.degree: PhD in Applied Mathematics and Machine Learning
education.detail: Thesis: “Optimal transport in high dimension: obtaining regularity and robustness using convexity and projections”, advised by Marco Cuturi.

education.degree: MSc in Statistics and Machine Learning (Mathématiques de l'Aléatoire, M2)
education.detail: Advanced courses in theoretical statistics and machine learning.
Specialized in statistics and data science.
Applied mathematics, with a focus on statistics, probability and data analysis.
Intensive mathematics and physics, preparing for the competitive entrance examinations to the Grandes Écoles.

publications.title: Algorithms for Weak Optimal Transport with an Application to Economics
publications.authors: F-P. Paty, P. Choné, F. Kramarz

publications.title: Optimal transport in high dimension: obtaining regularity and robustness using convexity and projections
publications.venue: PhD thesis, Institut Polytechnique de Paris

publications.title: Regularized Optimal Transport is Ground Cost Adversarial
Regularity as Regularization: Smooth and Strongly Convex Brenier Potentials in Optimal Transport
publications.authors: F-P. Paty, A. d'Aspremont, M. Cuturi
publications.venue: International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics (AISTATS)
publications.award: Notable Paper Award (top 3 of 423 accepted papers)

awards.text: Notable Paper Award (top 3 of 423 accepted papers), International Conference on Artificial Intelligence and Statistics (AISTATS).
Oral presentation (top 20% of accepted papers), International Conference on Machine Learning (ICML).
Congratulations of the Applied Mathematics department, École polytechnique.

teaching.org: École Nationale des Ponts et Chaussées
entries: Invited course on operations research.
Mathematics (in French): Topology and Functional Analysis (BSc); Differentiable Optimization (BSc); Mathematical Statistics (MSc).

Computer science (in English): Geometric Methods in Machine Learning (MSc); Stochastic Optimization and Automatic Differentiation for ML (MSc); Optimal Transport (MSc); Deep Learning: Models and Optimization (MSc).

service: Co-organizer of the Statistics, Econometrics, Machine Learning (Stat·Eco·ML) seminar, ENSAE Paris, 2019–2021.

skills.detail: Time series and forecasting, optimal transport, probabilistic models (HMM), deep learning, LLMs and generative AI.
MILP, dynamic programming, MDPs, operations research.
Python (JAX, scikit-learn, PyTorch, cvxpy), SQL, Git, CI/CD, React, Claude Code.
